entry.title: Re: Seamless Synergy: Forging SAP's Business Transformation capabilities with SAP Signavio and LeanI
entry.content:
I've looked into this integration possibility really hard.

When looking at it from a modern EA (TOGAF 10, SAFe, BizBOK) perspective I see a lot of IT centered architecture being implemented in stead of Enterprise Architecture.


I am missing key definitions and clarifications regarding the definitions SAP holds.
EA is all about being system agnostic. Shouldn't the SAP ecosystem reflect that?

It would be nice to get them aligned so we talk about the same things:

ElementLayerDescriptionMaster data tool 
Value StreamStrategicEnd-to-end flow of value from a customer trigger to business outcome. Represents cross-functional value deliveryLeanIX
Value ChainTacticalFunctional decomposition of how value is created within major business domains (e.g., Procurement, Sales, Operations).SAP Signavio
Business ProcessesOperational (Design & Modelling)BPMN or EPC representation of task-level workflows, responsibilities, decisions, rules, and system integrationsSAP Signavio
User stories, Requirements, Solution DesignOperational (Execution and monitoring)Drives project delivery and operationalizes approved designs/processes.SAP Cloud ALM
Business Capability Strategy

What the business does (or needs to do), independent of how it is implemented. (technology agnostic)

LeanIX
Solution Capability How (part of) a system is assigned to the business capability. It is what SAP uses in their reference catalog as capabilitySAP Ecosystem

When then looking into the integration options Value Chains should be a separate subtype of Business Context in LeanIX where SAP Signavio is leading.
From an EA perspective there should not be a direct link between a Value Chain and a Value Stream

Hierarchical mapping then should be:
Value stream - (served by relation) - Business Capability - (used by relation) - Value Chain - (aggregated by relation) - Business Process

==============================================================================

The term "Capability"  SAP uses in the SAP reference catalog context describes a functional domein enabled by a SAP solution, which are not business capabilities from Enterprise Architecture standards like TOGAF or BIZBOK. 

Mapping then should be:
A Business Capability - (realized by relation) - Solution Capability - (realized by relation) - Business Process - (served by relation) - Business Application (or Microservice)

It would be great to discuss/ reflect!

entry.title: Re: Possibility to add a button in "Change Maintenance Or...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

there are some extension capabilities offered, see the official documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/bb048da3cf164f4c8f98c2f2c9b7caa7.html

 

But what you might need, especially with a new button could go beyond that. So a side-by-side app, where you develop your own "Change Maintenance Order" app would be a solution.

 

There are APIs available, this one could be one you are looking for: https://api.sap.com/api/CE_API_MAINTENANCEORDER_0002/overview

 

So you basically build a UI on top of this as a side-by-side app running on BTP.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY using RAP ( Unmanaged )
entry.content:
Hi and thx a lot for clarifying this. 
Your approach runs properly if fired out of gw client, in a deep debug i also saw, that the system extracts the data in order to add an  $expand "to_Items" as second line in a request ( class /IWFND/CLLSODATA_PROCESSOR, method "create entity", table lt_parameter ).
Meaning, I debugged my client code in JS, copied the payload into gw client and started the debug. 

HOWEVER, I can do, what I want, I am not able to succeed if calling it from my JS ui5 code on my v2 odata model.

Whatever I do, in the backend in the method "deep_ceate" the items table NEVER gets filled, only the header table.

My call 

  // TO TEST: **bleep** DEEP INSERT DOES NOT WORK IN THE BACKEND, ITEMS ARE LOST 
                    //
                        let orderToCreate = {
                            Matnr:     head.Matnr, 
                            Gtin:      head.Gtin, 
                            Lifnr:     head.Lifnr,
                            Field1:    f1,
                            ...
                            ..
                            .
                            to_Items: _newItems
                        };                        
                    //
                    // TO TEST: -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                    //
                    return await new Promise((resolve, reject) => {                                                                     
                                      oDataModel.createEntry("/MY_TEST_ORDER", { properties:orderToCreate ,
                                                                   created:
                                                                   function(Params) {

                                                                        resolve(oDataModel);
                                                                                
                                                                    },                                                                    
                                                                    error:
                                                                    function(oError) {
                                                                        reject(console.log(oError.toString()));
                                                                    },                                                                    
                                                                });
                    });


All works as I thought, it SHOULD work. No dump, no errors, BUT the item table is always empty.

The navigation property is published and is exposed, I can see it in the metadata inside the web-ide of the  bas.
Why are the items not passed properly from JS ?

Maybe I need to republish and then refresh the metadata inside bas? 


Thx for any clarification.

entry.title: Unlocking the Key Determinations in SAP SD
entry.content:
Understanding SAP SD Determinations: The Backbone of Seamless Transactions:
In every SAP SD implementation, determination procedures play a vital role in ensuring accurate and automated processes across sales, delivery, and billing. In this blog, I’ve compiled the most critical determinations—like pricing, tax, route, shipping point, plant, and more—along with configuration steps, key T-codes, and practical error-resolution tips to help consultants and end users alike.                                

1. Revenue Account Determination = Application +Condition Type+ Chart of Accounts + Sales Organization +Customer Account Assignment Group (In customer master Billing Tab) + Material Account Assignment Group (In material master sales org 2 view) + Account Key  

T-code: VKOA

Error: Revenue account posting fails in billing

Cause:

  • Condition type not assigned to correct account key in V/08.
  • Maintain missing GL Account for relevant key in VKOA.

2. Pricing Determination = Sales area+ Document pricing procedure in (VOV8) + Customer pricing procedure in (Customer Master sales Tab) + Condition type

T-code: OVKK

                             

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Error: Pricing procedure not determined in Sales Order

Cause:

  • Missing or incorrect assignment in OVKK (Pricing Procedure Determination).
  • Missing access sequence in pricing condition type (V/06).

3. Tax Determination = Destination country of the Ship to party + Departure country of the Shipping point+ Tax classification for customer master (Billing Tab in CM) + Tax Classification for Material Master (Material Master-Sales org 1 view)

T-code: OVK1

                    

                   

Error: Tax is not calculated, or incorrect tax amount appears in the invoice.
Cause:

  • Tax classification missing in Customer Master (XD01)
  • Tax classification missing in Material Master (MM01)

4. Route Determination = Shipping conditions (in customer master shipping tab) + Transport Group (sales general/Plant view in MM) + Destination Zone of the Ship to party + Departure Zone of the Shipping point.  T-code: OVL4

                                   

 

 

 

 

 

 

Maintain Route Determination

Error: Incorrect or missing route in the sales order.

 Cause:

  • Transportation zone not maintained in Customer Master
  • Route determination is not configured in OVL4.

5. Plant Determination= While System pulls Plant into the order, firstly it checks at CMIR level. Maintain Plant in T-code VD51        

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

If the plant is maintained there, the plant will be determined into sales document, otherwise system checks for plant at customer Master level.

      

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

If the plant is maintained there, plant will be determined into sales document Automatically, otherwise system checks for plant at material master level.

            

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

If Plant is not available at material master level also, we must maintain plant manually.

T-code: OVL2

Determines from which plant the goods will be delivered.

Error: The system fails to determine the plant in a sales order.

Cause:

  • Material Master (MM02 - Sales Org 1 View) lacks plant assignment.
  • Customer Material Info Record (VD51) missing plant assignment.
  • Customer Master (XD03 - Sales Area Data) does not have a default plant

6. Shipping Point Determination= Plant+ Shipping Conditions (in Customer master shipping Tab) +Loading Group (sales general/plant view in MM)

  T-code: OVL2

      

                                 

Error: Incorrect or missing shipping point in the delivery.
Cause:

  • Shipping conditions are missing in Customer Master (XD03 - Sales Area Data).
  • Loading Group missing in Material Master (MM02 - Sales Org 1 View).
  • Plant-Shipping Point assignment not maintained in OVL2.

7. Storage Location Determination= Storage Conditions (In MM Plant Data/ Storage 1 View) + Plant + Shipping Conditions (Shipping Tab in Customer Master)

T-code: OVL3

Storage location gets determined at delivery level and Maintain Determine storage location should be Checked in Delivery Item Category (OVLP).

Storage location gets Determined based on 3 rules

  • In OVLK, if Storage location Rule assigned to Delivery Type is MALA, System Considers

Storage Conditions + Shipping Point + Plant

                  

  •  In OVLK, if Storage location Rule assigned to Delivery Type is RETA, System Considers

Storage Conditions + situation + Plant

  • In OVLK, if Storage location Rule assigned to Delivery Type is MARE, System Considers

Firstly, the System considers MALA to determine storage location, if it is not possible it will choose RETA as Back up Procedure

Determine the correct Storage Location for picking goods.

Error: Storage location is not automatically picked in the delivery.
 Cause:

  • Storage location not maintained in Material Master
  • Storage location determination missing in OVL3 (Plant & Shipping Point  assignment)

8. Delivery Item Category Determination= Delivery Type + Item Category Group +Item Usage + Higher level Item Category.

T-code: 0814

It is used while creating a Delivery Document without Sales order. By Default, there is Standard order type (DL), item category (DLN), schedule line (CP) and Delivery type (LO) available in the system by SAP.

9. Item Category Determination= Sales Document Type +Item Category Group + Item Usage+ Higher Level Item Category.

T-code: VOV4

System Considers Item usage and higher-level Item category in scenarios like          

  • Cross-selling
  • Material Determination
  • Free Goods Determination
  • BOM

The reason being that all the above scenarios have subline items determined into the sales Document and to determine Item category to the subline items system considers item usage and higher-level item category

Error: missing item category in the sales order.
Cause:

  • Incorrect assignment in VOV4 (Item Category Determination).
  • Item category group missing in Material Master (MM02 – Sales Org 2 View).

10. Scheduled Line Category Determination= Item category + MRP Type of the material.

T-code: VOV5

Error: Schedule line category missing or incorrect confirmed quantity in sales order.
Cause:

  • Schedule Line category not mapped in VOV5.
  • MRP type missing in Material Master (MM02 - MRP 1 View).                                                      

11. Delivery Document Determination = it is achieved through Maintaining Delivery type in Corresponding Sales Document Type in VOV8

When a sales order is created, the system checks the assigned delivery type and automatically determines the relevant delivery document in VL01N.

12. Billing Document Determination = it is achieved through Maintaining Billing type in Corresponding Sales Document Type in VOV8

When a sales order is created, the system checks the assigned Billing type and automatically determines the relevant Billing document in VF01.

Conclusion

Determinations in SAP SD automate business processes by ensuring accurate data flow across sales, delivery, and billing, reducing manual effort and errors. They enable seamless integration between sales, logistics, and finance, enhancing efficiency and compliance. Proper configuration ensures smooth transaction execution, improving accuracy and customer satisfaction.

entry.title: Account Assignment Objects in Asset Master Data in Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA)
entry.content:
Purpose

In UPA, Asset Master Data supports various account assignment objects. They all appear in the time-dependent data (time-interval) of an asset master record. 

As Asset Accounting (AA) has a lot of integrations with other applications, the way that AA handles account assignment objects is very complex. In the past, even inconsistent data can be stored into database.

In order to avoid these situations, AA in UPA has some restrictions in account assignment objects to ensure the data consistency. This article describes the most important restrictions and their backgrounds.

Account Assignment Objects

Account Assignment Objects are used to collect costs or expenses. Cost center, profit center and segment are most widely used objects. In most cases, they are marked as 'Active' in the view V_FAA_ACCTASN, as shown in the screenshot below. And then, they can be used in the asset master records.

Profit Center and Segment are usually marked as 'Relevant to Balance Sheet' in addition. Because they are often used to show asset balance in profit center / segment reportings. As a result, they are mandatory fields in asset master data.

Time-Dependent Data of Asset Master Data

Account Assignment Objects appear in the section of time-dependent data (time-interval) in asset master record.

When you create a new asset master record, the profit center is derived from the cost center or WBS element, and then the segment from the profit center. Therefore, although they appear to be read-only by default, system ensures they must have values. This derivation also ensures the cost center and its profit center are always consistently matching.

After saving this master data as shown in the screenshot above, the cost center 101011752 and the profit center YB600 can be used to collect depreciation expense.

⚠️Hint: You can add multiple time-intervals at a time on creation. If some of the intervals contain exactly the same values, (e.g. same cost center, same profit center, same plant, and even same room, etc.), after saving the asset master, they will be merged into one single time-interval.

Restrictions in CO

Cost Center is a time-dependent CO object. The same cost center can belong to different profit centers over different periods of time.

In asset master data, they are time-dependent as well. To avoid confusing the end users, both AA and CO have restrictions in the object definition in UPA:

  • Once a cost center has multiple time-intervals with assignments to different profit centers, it cannot be used in asset master data anymore.
  • Vice versa. Once a cost center has been assigned to an asset master record, in CO the cost center cannot have one more time-interval with assignment to a different profit center.

Restrictions in WBS Element

An asset under construction (AuC) can be generated from a WBS element. In the definition of a WBS element, you can enter cost center and profit center too. Unfortunately, even today, you can still enter invalid combination of cost center and profit center in your WBS element.

For example, you have defined the following CO objects.

Cost CenterProfit Center
APC1
BPC2

It is obvious that cost center A and profit center PC2 should not work together. However, you can still enter this meaningless combination (cost center A and profit center PC2) in your WBS element.

When an AuC is created from this WBS element, AA recognizes this inconsistency. As the consequence, only the profit center PC2 is taken over to the master record of AuC, but without cost center being filled in the AuC. ➡️ This is because AA cannot tell which one is intended by the end user. Profit center is taken, simply because it is marked as 'Relevant to Balance Sheet' and might be 'more correct'.

You can go back to your WBS element, to correct your cost center and profit center. And they will be adjusted in the asset master record of the AuC.

⚠️Hint: If you are changing the cost center / project center in your WBS element, it can happen that the your changes do not reach the asset master record of the AuC. It is mostly likely that the combination of cost center and profit center is wrong. Please check it in your WBS element.

Restrictions in AA

You will have these restrictions especailly when your asset has documents posted.

First, you can only change an existing time-interval, if it is the only or last time-interval. The reason behind is simple. Imagine you have two time-intervals, first one with profit center PC1 and the second with PC2. That means asset balance has been transferred from PC1 to PC2 already. You should not go to the first time-interval to change the profit center and destroy the consistency.

Second, you are not always allowed to change the cost center in an existing time-interval. To make it simpler to explain, let's say it is the only time-interval in your master record. And there, you want to change your cost center from A to B.

If both cost centers ares assigned to the same profit center PC1 (like the following), this change is OK. That is because this asset always stays with profit center PC1. Asset balance for PC1 is not affected.

Cost CenterProfit Center
APC1
BPC1

On the contrary, if the two cost centers belong to different profit centers, as shown in the table below, system will reject the change with the error message FAA_MD122.

Cost CenterProfit Center
APC1
BPC2

This error message tells you why this change is not allowed. Imagine: if the system had allowed the change, what would happen? The asset master record would show you that it had always stayed with the profit center PC2 from the beginning. However, in the asset reporting, you would see asset balance used to stay with profit center PC1.

Summary

All these restrictions and validations are designed to prevent inconsistent data from sneaking into the database.

If you want to collect your costs into a different profit center, always add a new time-interval and change the profit center there. Then, after your saving master data, asset balance will be transferred to the new profit center with a transfer document.

⚠️ Hint: for more details about transfer asset balance from within asset master record, refer to the blog posting here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/account-assignment-changes-and-follow-up-postings-in-universal-parallel/ba-p/14114182

entry.title: Account Assignment Change and Follow-Up Posting in Asset Master Data in UPA
entry.content:
Purpose

In the past, Account Assignment Change was known as Re-organization in Asset Master Data. It is used to transfer asset balance to a different profit center / segment. 

This article describes this feature in Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA), with a concrete example, focusing on the improvements since its earlier version.

⚠️Hint: For more information about the account assignment objects in time-dependent data of asset master record, please refer to this page: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/account-assignment-objects-in-asset-master-data-in-universal-parallel/ba-p/14114128 

Example

In this example,

  • The company code has 2 open fiscal years: 2024 & 2025.
  • The asset has an acquisition on Jan 1st of 2024, with amount 12,000 EUR. 
  • The depreciation amount in each period is 100 EUR.

This asset has two time-intervals.

  • The first one is assigned to the profit center YB600, up to Jan 31 of 2024.
  • Next, you want to add the 2nd time-interval starts from Feb 1st of 2024, with the profit center YB700.

Limitations in Early Days

In the early days, the so-called re-organization shall be executed in exactly the following steps. 

  • Post depreciation up to the period right before the re-organization.
    In this example, you must post depreciation in period 1 first. This is to ensure all asset transactions under YB600 are fully booked in FI documents.
  • Add the new time-interval with the re-organization.
    In this example, add the new time-interval starting from Feb 1st of 2024, with the profit center YB700.
    By doing this, the complete asset balance is transferred from YB600 to YB700.
  • Post depreciation in period 2
    Now, the depreciation will be posted to YB700.

Limitation 1: You cannot trigger the re-organization without posting depreciation in period 1. That is because asset transactions under YB600 haven't been fully booked yet.

Limitation 2: After the re-organization, you cannot post any document back in period 1. Otherwise, the balance in previous profit center YB600 cannot remain zero anymore. ➡️ And that is why you must post depreciation in period 1 beforehand.

Limitation 3: Re-organization can only happen in 1st open fiscal year. ➡️ Otherwise, you have to face the problems mentioned in 'Limitation 2'.

Account Assignment Change and Follow-up Posting Today

With the improvements in UPA, all limitations above have been removed.

You can make account assignment change without posting depreciation in period 1 beforehand.

That is because after account assignment change, the depreciation postings in period 1 can be automatically transferred to the YB700.

With that, the limitation 3 disappears automatically. You can make account assignment change in the upper open fiscal year too.

⚠️Hint: Your new time-interval with the account assignment change shall not cover any existing postings. In this example, if there is any posting existing on / after Feb 1st of 2024, you are not allowed to trigger this account assignment change. ➡️ The reason is clear: these existing documents have been posted to the previous profit center YB600. If you want to transfer all the balance from YB600, you need a new time-interval with a later starting date. 

Summary

The improvements in Account Assignment Change in UPA benefit customers a lot, with all limitation from early days removed.

One thing worth mentioning here is about how to cancel the account assignment change. That means you don't want to have any balance under YB700. Instead, you want to go back to YB600.

  • The first thing you must make sure that there is no document posted after the date of account assignment change, i.e. no new transactions for YB700. 
  • If there is any, reverse them.
  • Now, as the last step, delete the time-interval for this account assignment change.

⚠️Hint: To reversel normal asset postings, you can simply do it by clicking on the document number in 'Value Display' of Manage Fixed Assets app. And then, click on 'Reverse Journal Entries - In Asset Accounting' from the context menu. However, to revese an account assignment change, you must delete the corresponding time-interval for this account assignment change. This is the only way.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2025 Compensation Releases: Enhancements to Statements
entry.content:
Introduction:

As organizations continue to evolve in their approach to employee compensation, SAP SuccessFactors remains at the forefront of providing innovative solutions that enhance the employee experience. The 1H 2025 Compensation releases introduce significant updates, particularly in compensation statements. This blog will explore the key enhancements and their implications for HR professionals and employees alike.

Understanding Compensation Statements:

Compensation statements are vital tools for communicating an employee's total rewards package. They provide transparency regarding salary, bonuses, benefits, and other forms of compensation. Effective compensation statements not only help employees understand their value within the organization but also play a crucial role in retention and engagement.

Key Enhancements to Compensation Statements in 1H 2025:

1.     Employee Central Data on Statement Template:

In the ever-evolving landscape of HR technology, the ability to streamline processes and enhance user experience is paramount. One of the latest enhancements in the Employee Central data integration allows organizations to display Employee Central data directly on compensation statements without the need to include those fields in the compensation plan. Below are the details of this enhancement, its technical aspects, and the benefits it brings to HR professionals.

What's Changed:

Before this enhancement, if HR professionals wanted to show fields from Employee Central on compensation statements, they had to include those fields in the compensation plan. This added complexity to the compensation planning process and required ongoing maintenance of the fields in the template. However, with the recent update, users can now directly link Employee Central fields to the statement template, simplifying the process significantly.

How It Looks Now

In the current version, users can choose between "Plan" and "Employee Central" when adding relevant fields to the statement template. This flexibility allows for a more tailored approach to compensation statements, ensuring that only the necessary information is displayed.

How It Looked Before

Previously, configuring compensation templates required the use of the "Add Compensation Field" button to incorporate fields from the compensation plan template into the statement template. This method was not only cumbersome but also led to a more complex compensation plan.

Technical Details:

The enhancement supports various categories and data types from Employee Central, allowing for a more comprehensive view of employee information on compensation statements. Here are the key technical details:

Supported Employee Central Categories:

  • Job Information
  • Compensation Information
  • Employment Details

Supported Data Types:

  • Date
  • Boolean
  • String
  • Localized
  • Translatable
  • Legacy Picklists

Not Supported Data Types:

Certain data types are not displayed in the "Add Field" dropdown selector, including:

  • Number
  • Amount
  • Percent
  • Localized values from MDF Picklists and other data types from Configure Object Definition.
  • While MDF picklists appear in the dropdown selector, only the default value is supported.

Additionally, root base objects cannot be selected; only child fields under the object can be added.

The data from the Employee Central will be shown as per the Template’s effective date.

Benefits of the Enhancement:

This enhancement brings several advantages to HR professionals and organizations:

  1. Simplified Process: By allowing direct linking of Employee Central fields to statement templates, the complexity of maintaining fields in the compensation plan is reduced.
  2. Flexibility: Organizations can now change statement templates even after the compensation planning process has begun, without disrupting ongoing processes.
  3. Streamlined Compensation Plans: With the ability to exclude unnecessary Employee Central fields from the compensation plan, HR professionals can create cleaner and more efficient plans.
  4. Improved User Experience: The new functionality enhances the overall user experience by providing a more intuitive and flexible way to manage compensation statements.

The integration of Employee Central data into compensation statements marks a significant step forward in HR technology. By simplifying the process and enhancing flexibility, organizations can create more effective compensation plans that better reflect employee contributions. As HR continues to evolve, such enhancements will play a crucial role in driving efficiency and improving the overall employee experience.

 

2.     Template Validation and PDF Preview for Rewards Statement Templates:

In today’s landscape of employee rewards and recognition, ensuring accuracy and clarity in rewards statements is paramount. To enhance the user experience and minimize errors, we are excited to introduce significant updates to our statement editor. Below are the details of the new features of template validation and PDF preview for rewards statement templates, outlining their technical details and related information.

Technical Details:

Enhanced Template Validation:

One of the most notable enhancements is the introduction of template validation when importing custom templates as XSL files in the statement editor. When users navigate to the "Add Template" section and select "Import File" for XSL, the system now conducts a thorough validation of the template code.

This means that if the imported file is not a valid XSL template, the import process will be halted, preventing any erroneous files from being uploaded. In previous versions, the validation was limited, allowing files with errors to be imported, which could lead to complications down the line. This improvement not only streamlines the import process but also significantly reduces the likelihood of errors in the final rewards statements.

PDF Preview Feature:

In addition to template validation, we are thrilled to introduce a new feature: the ability to preview the PDF for rewards statements directly within the statement editor. This feature allows users to visualize the final appearance of the PDF statements before they are generated for employees.

The "Download Sample PDF" action is now available for both custom and standard rewards statement templates. Users can easily access this option alongside other functionalities such as Export, Delete, and Preview in a newly organized column of Actions on the far right of the statement editor.

How It Looks Now

How It Looks before

This reorganization not only enhances usability but also ensures that users can quickly find the actions they need without confusion.

These enhancements are designed to empower users by providing them with the tools necessary to create accurate and visually appealing rewards statements. By validating templates upon import and allowing for PDF previews, users can catch and correct potential issues before generating statements for their employees.

In conclusion, the updates to the rewards statement templates—template validation and PDF preview—represent a significant step forward in reducing errors and improving the overall user experience. We believe these enhancements will lead to more accurate rewards statements, fostering greater trust and satisfaction among employees.

 

3.     Automatic Recall of Compensation Statements Now Configurable:

Organizations often find themselves needing to adjust completed compensation worksheets, which can involuntarily trigger the automatic recall of reward statements. However, with the latest updates, companies can now configure their systems to prevent this automatic recall, enhancing their operational efficiency and adaptability.

Configuration Requirements:

To take advantage of this new feature, users must navigate to the Compensation Home section. From there, follow these steps:

  1. Click on Actions for All Plans.
  2. Select Company Settings.
  3. In the Compensation and Variable Pay section, locate the setting labelled Enable recall of statements when completed worksheets are routed back to in progress status.

By default, this setting is checked, meaning that the system will automatically recall reward statements when worksheets are reverted to in-progress status. If your organization prefers to maintain the generated statements, simply uncheck this box. This change allows for a more tailored approach to managing compensation statements, aligning with specific business needs.

What's Changed:

The primary enhancement in this version is the ability to prevent the recall of associated compensation reward statements when worksheets transition from completed to in-progress status. In previous versions, this transition would automatically trigger the recall of all reward statements linked to the worksheet, which could lead to confusion and administrative burdens.

With the new configuration option, organizations can now manage their compensation processes with greater precision. This change is particularly beneficial in scenarios where the recall of all statements is not desirable, allowing for a more streamlined approach to compensation management.

Technical Details:

The configuration works seamlessly across various types of worksheets, including compensation, variable pay, and total compensation worksheets. When compensation worksheets are moved back to in-progress status, users can still view the number of reward statements generated for completed worksheets through the Compensation Home > Complete Compensation Cycle > Rewards Statements > Manage Statement Templates screen.

It’s important to note that when worksheets are reverted to in-progress status, the displayed number of employees assigned to a statement template reflects only those for completed worksheets. To verify the existence of a Rewards Statement for an employee in this scenario, users can check the People Profile.

This new configuration capability not only enhances user control but also supports various business scenarios where flexibility is essential. Organizations can now adapt their compensation management processes to better fit their operational needs without the risk of unintended consequences from automatic recalls.

In conclusion, the ability to configure the automatic recall of compensation statements marks a significant improvement in compensation management systems. By allowing organizations to maintain control over their reward statements, this feature fosters a more efficient and adaptable approach to managing compensation processes. Embrace this change to enhance your organization’s compensation strategy and ensure that it aligns with your unique business requirements.

 

4.     Enhancements to the Personal Compensation Statement Generation Notification:

In today's fast-paced business environment, effective communication is key to maintaining employee engagement and satisfaction. One of the critical aspects of this communication is the Personal Compensation Statement Generation notification. Recent enhancements to this feature have made it easier for organizations to tailor their messaging, ensuring that employees receive clear and visually appealing information regarding their compensation.

What's Changed:

The latest updates to the Email Notification Templates Settings -> Personal Compensation Statement Generation notification bring significant improvements to the email template customization process. Users can now configure the email template to include HTML, allowing for a more polished and professional appearance. This enhancement means that organizations can now incorporate a header, footer, and their company logo into the email, creating a cohesive brand experience for employees.

Additionally, organizations can create separate email templates based on different compensation plan templates. This flexibility allows for more personalized communication, catering to the specific needs of various employee groups and compensation structures.

Technical Details:

The logo used in the email notification is pulled directly from the Company System and Logo Settings tool. This integration ensures that the branding remains consistent across all communications, reinforcing the company's identity.

How It Looks Now

With the new features, the Personal Compensation Statement Generation notification email now boasts a more professional layout. The inclusion of the company logo at both the header and footer of the email creates a visually appealing presentation. Employees will appreciate the effort put into making the communication not only informative but also aesthetically pleasing.

How It Looked Before

In the previous version, the limitations of the email template were evident. Organizations were unable to create multiple email templates based on different form templates, which restricted their ability to communicate effectively with diverse employee groups. The lack of customization options meant that many employees received generic notifications that did not resonate with their specific compensation plans.

In conclusion, the enhancements to the Personal Compensation Statement Generation notification are a significant step forward in improving employee communication. By allowing for HTML customization, the inclusion of company branding, and the ability to create tailored email templates, organizations can now deliver a more engaging and informative experience to their employees. As businesses continue to evolve, these improvements will play a crucial role in fostering a positive workplace culture and enhancing employee satisfaction.

 

5.     Deletion of Homepage Cards Upon Recall of Compensation Statements:

In the compensation management, ensuring clarity and accuracy in communication is important. One of the recent enhancements that address this need is the Deletion of Homepage Cards associated with recalled Rewards Statements. This change streamlines the user experience by ensuring that outdated or incorrect information is promptly removed from view.

Configuration Requirements:

To utilize this feature, users can recall rewards statements through the following navigation path:

  1. Navigate to Compensation Home.
  2. Select the Template Name.
  3. Proceed to Complete Compensation Cycle.
  4. Click on Rewards Statements.
  5. Finally, choose Recall Statements.

This straightforward process allows for efficient management of compensation statements, ensuring that only the most relevant information is displayed.

What's Changed:

In the latest version of the system, a significant change has been implemented: Homepage cards associated with recalled rewards statements are now deleted from view. This is a notable improvement over the previous version, where these cards remained visible even after the statements were recalled.

Previous Version:

In the earlier iteration, users often encountered confusion as the homepage cards linked to recalled rewards statements continued to appear. This could lead to misunderstandings and miscommunication regarding the status of compensation statements, ultimately affecting employee trust and satisfaction.

Current Version:

With the current update, the system automatically deletes the homepage cards when the associated rewards statements are recalled, whether this recall occurs automatically due to template routing or manually by the user. This ensures that employees and managers alike have access to accurate and up-to-date information, fostering a more transparent compensation process.

Technical Details:

The technical implementation of this feature involves a seamless integration within the existing compensation management framework. By linking the recall action directly to the visibility of homepage cards, the system can efficiently manage the display of information in real-time. This not only enhances user experience but also reduces the potential for errors in compensation communication.

Conclusion:

The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2025 Compensation releases bring exciting enhancements to compensation statements, focusing on customization, user experience, real-time data integration, reporting capabilities, and compliance. These updates not only empower HR professionals but also enhance the overall employee experience. As organizations continue to navigate the complexities of compensation management, these tools will be invaluable in fostering a transparent and engaging workplace culture.

SAP SuccessFactors Compensation

entry.title: Outbound ASN for Consignment Order
entry.content:
Suppose we order some item from Amazon or any other e-commerce platform, we receive notification of items shipped based on our communication preference, and it is called ASN in SAP terminology.

Here I am listing the step-by-step configuration required for the same.

Assumption: Types are defined for Freight Unit, Freight Order and Consignment Order.

Apart from the Freight order and consignment order, major part is PPF configuration.

  1. PPF Adapter setting
  2. Define action profile
  3. Use the standard class, this is the class which contains the method for ASN.
  4. Action Definition

  5. Processing Type - select the processing type as a Method Call
  6. Maintain Output Management Adapter Settings
  7. Maintain Output Agent
  8. Assign PPF profile
  9. Action Settings
  10. Assign the action profile to the output profile of the consignment order
  11. Create consignment building profile: this will create a consignment order when FO status is changed to “Ready for Transport Execution”.
  12. Configure Webservice TransportationOrderGenericRequest_Out under namespace http://sap.com/xi/TMS/Global using the endpoint provided by BTP/CPI team.

Testing outbound ASN depends on scenario configuration. List of steps that we had to follow are:

  1. Create a sales order and freight units are automatically created.
  2. Plan the freight units onto the freight order either manually or using optimizer. If created manually, then assign the carrier.
  3. Create delivery for the freight order.
  4. Set the execution status of the freight order to “Ready for Transport Execution”
  5. From the Overview tab select an action to “Set to Ready for Warehouse Processing”
  6. Based on the outbound delivery order, create the Picking and Staging warehouse tasks and confirm both the warehouse tasks.
  7. Set the ODO to “Set to Ready for Shipping"
  8. Perform following actions using “Load or Unload Freight Order app”
    1. Assign the warehouse door
    2. Set Items to Loaded
    3. Set Stop to Unloading Completed
  9. Post Goods Movement
    This action will trigger the Outbound ASN which will be visible in the “Communication History” tab of the CO and we can check status of sent messages in SRT_MONI.

Thanks for reading my blog. Hope this helps someone looking for steps of configurations. Please share your feedback.

Best Regards

Shivang

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud for Manufacturing: Streamlining Supply Chains
entry.content:
In this blog we will see about SAP Public Cloud for Manufacturing: Streamlining Supply Chain as follow,

How SAP Public cloud help Supply chain manufacturing Industries:

SAP S/4hana Cloud for Supply Chain Management in public clouds provides a wide, integrated platform that supports each phase of the supply chain from an execution plan on a real -time basis. It gives production and distribution companies the right to work with greater transparency, agility and efficiency.

Key capabilities include demand forecasting to better anticipate customer needs, inventory management for optimal stock levels, transportation optimization to reduce logistics costs, and order fulfilment to ensure timely and accurate deliveries. These capabilities are tightly integrated, ensuring smooth data flow across departments such as procurement, production, warehouse, and sales.

The solution uses automation to reduce manual functions and errors, streamline processes through intelligent workflows and support real -time decision-making with built- in analyses and alerts. This allows companies to require offers, disruption or bottleneck supply and demand, because it's cloud-based, businesses benefit from lower infrastructure and maintenance costs, faster deployment, and quarterly updates that continuously bring new innovations without lengthy upgrade cycles.

Overall, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud helps supply chain-driven companies improve service levels, reduce operational costs, and remain competitive in a fast-changing market.

Functionality of Supply chain in public cloud:

SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud offers end-to-end supply chain capabilities, enabling manufacturing industries to plan, execute, and monitor their operations efficiently.

Key functionalities:

  1. Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP): To ensure the reliable delivery dates Real- time product availability checks are performed.
  2. Inventory & Warehouse Management: In Inventory & Warehouse Management Real-time stock visibility, batch/serial tracking, and optimized warehouse operations.
  3. Production Planning & Execution: Efficient planning and control of manufacturing processes like MTO, MTS, etc.
  4. Demand & Supply Matching: Automated MRP and smart replenishment to balance inventory with customer demand.
  5. Procurement & Supplier Collaboration: Seamless sourcing, purchasing, and supplier communication.
  6.  Quality Management: During production and goods receipt Integrated quality check is performed.

Features that streamline the supply chain in public cloud:

  1. End-to-End Process Integration: seamless connection between sales, procurement, production, inventory, and logistics, this integration eliminates data silos, minimizes manual handovers, and ensures that every department has access to the same real-time information. hence, businesses can improve collaboration, reduce process delays, and enable smooth execution from order to delivery.
  2. Automated Workflows: Automates routine tasks like PO creation, delivery, goods movement, and invoice generation. This reduces manual errors, speeds up operations, and ensures consistent process execution.
  3. Embedded Analytics & KPIs: Built-in dashboards and analytical tools provide real-time insights into inventory levels, supplier performance, production efficiency, and order fulfillment rates. Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) are visually presented, enabling stakeholders to make informed decisions quickly. These analytics are embedded directly into the operational screens, so users don’t have to switch tools to get insights.
  4. Real-Time Data & Alerts: Instant visibility of sock level and delays, automated alerts notify users of critical supply chain as issue occurs.
  5. Demand-Driven Replenishment: The system automatically adjusts inventory levels thus prevents and reduces overstock and shortages for forecast-based planning.
  6. User-Friendly Fiori Apps: Easy to use screens in Fiori apps, these apps are accessible on both desktop and mobile devices, making it easy to perform the tasks.

Implementing SAP Public cloud solutions in supply chain management typically leads to the following improvements:

  1. Increased Transparency: Real-time data and full transparency from the source to the end customers make it easy to monitor their behaviour. It empowers decision-makers with live insights to respond quickly to changes and improve supply chain responsiveness.
  2. Improved Efficiency: Less manual involvement and mistakes are caused by automated monotonous tasks and refined processes. Streamlined workflows ensure faster processing times, better coordination between departments, and fewer delays. As a result, overall operational efficiency improves significantly.
  3. Scalability: It is easy to scale up or down any cloud-based services according to business needs. Whether a company is expanding into new markets or downsizing, resources can be adjusted dynamically without major infrastructure changes
  4. Better Togetherness: This unified platform enhances how suppliers, partners, and customers work together. This tight integration enhances supplier coordination, improves customer service, and ensures everyone in the network operates with the same set of up-to-date information.
  5. Advanced Analytics: Sophisticated data analytics leads to better predictions and more personalized reports; this data- driven approach enhances planning accuracy and supports smarter decision- making.
  6. Cost Reduction: This model saves on IT infrastructure by charging only when necessary.
  7. Improved Compliance and Security Measures: SAP ensures high levels of data security and regulatory compliance, with built-in controls that support global standards and industry-specific regulations.
  8. Faster Implementation Time: Rapid deployment speeds enable seamless upgrades, with quarterly updates and pre-configured best practices, organizations can implement, adapt, and upgrade the solution faster than traditional on-premise systems.
  9. Greater Customer Satisfaction Levels: Enhanced delivery of orders in time and a better understanding of customers contribute to their happiness ensuring higher loyalty and satisfaction.

Public Cloud – Supply Chain Module specific key highlights:

  1. SD (Sales & Distribution): It Manages sales orders with aATP checks and seamless order- to- cash.
  2. PP (Production Planning): It Plans and executes MTO/MTS production processes and planning of material and capacity in Real- time.
  3. MM (Materials Management): Can create purchase requisitions and order and Automates procurement with supplier collaboration.
  4. EWM (Warehouse Management): Integrates with IM module and Bin-level tracking, putway, picking.
  5. FI (Financial Accounting): Real- time cost tracking for supply chain activities and automatic posting of GR, invoice receipts.
  6. Analytics (Embedded SAC): Real-time dashboards for inventory, delays, supplier performance.
  7. MRP (Demand Planning): Real-time MRP runs.
  8. Integration Across Modules: End-to-end visibility from procurement to delivery with Fiori apps

As this boosts supply chain efficiency through seamless integration of key modules (SD, MM, PP, EWM, FI), enabling automated workflows, real-time visibility, and data-driven decision-making. This reduces manual effort, minimizes errors, lowers costs, and accelerates responses across procurement, production, inventory, and logistics.

Real time example:

A generator engine manufacturing company receives custom orders but struggles with delays due to poor tracking of vendor parts and manual coordination between departments.

With the help of SAP Public cloud manufacturing business streamlines as follow:

  1. As Customer places an order, system checks material availability instantly.
  2. SAP auto-triggers a production order for in-house items and a purchase order for third-party parts.
  3. Vendor delivers directly to customer, and SAP handles billing without needing warehouse receipt.
  4. This all process is being managed in Fiori dashboard in real- time.

Thus, increased faster order delivery, less follow up manually, with on- time shipment.

entry.title: EWM & QM integration - Quality inspection after GR from production
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION
This guide will demonstrate how to set up and perform a quality inspection after goods receipt from production using EWM. The process involves the advanced production integration with EWM and the Quality Inspection Engine (QIE) on the EWM side.

PROCESS OVERVIEW
Let's consider a scenario in a hypothetical warehouse. The goods are produced, and the production team creates the handling unit using an RF device. The quality inspection must take place before the final putaway.

ASSUMPTIONS
We assume that the advanced production integration between EWM and PP is already in place. 

Step 1: Receiving HU with RFUI
The handling units are received in EWM. The user scans the production order and confirms the HU creation using standard menu 3464. After that, an inbound delivery is automatically created and should be visible at /SCWM/PRDI.

Step 2: Create WT to QM Work Center
Next, create a warehouse task (WT) to move the HU from the GR-PROD storage bin to the quality inspection work center.

Step 3: Process Usage Decision
Perform the usage decision using transaction code /SCWM/QINSP_S4. If the results are positive, a WT is created to move the HU to the final storage according to the follow-up actions.

Step 4: Confirm Final Putaway
Finally, confirm the WT to move the HU from the work center to the final storage bin.

CUSTOMIZING STEPS

QM steps:
- Generate Inspection Object Type Versions
- Maintain Inspection Object Types Version
- Maintain Inspection Rule Group
- Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs
- Define Follow-Up Actions
- Assign Logistical Follow-Up Actions
- Assign Default Work Center for Inspections 

EWM steps:
- Define Process-Oriented Storage Control
- Define Warehouse Process Type

Master data steps:
- Maintain material master - MM01)
- Define Master Data Attributes - /SCWM/TWORKST
- Define inspection rule - /SCWM/QRSETUP

All the steps above will be detailed in this blog. The following steps are necessary to set up the quality inspection process in EWM. First, generate the IOT objects:

  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Basics and Integration -> Generate Inspection Object Type Versions


  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Basics and Integration -> Maintain Inspection Object Types Version


  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Inspection Planning -> Maintain Inspection Rule Group

  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Basics and Integration -> Define and Activate Warehouse-Dependent IOTs


  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Inspection Results -> Maintain Follow-Up Actions - > Define Follow-Up Actions

    If you want to customize the follow-up actions, you can assign your function module with custom logic to meet your specific business requirements, especially if the standard processes are not sufficient.

  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Inspection Results -> Maintain Follow-Up Actions - > Assign Logistical Follow-Up Actions

    The customizing below will set the stock type to unrestricted “FF” after the usage decision (UD) is completed using transaction code /SCWM/QINSP_S4.

  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Quality Management -> Inspection Results -> Assign Default Work Center for Inspections


    After the goods receipt, a process-oriented storage control (POSC) will be executed to handle the quality inspection and final putaway. To enable this, the following steps must be in place:

  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Process-Oriented Storage Control

    Start defining the external steps that will be used and assign them to an internal process step as follows:

    Then create a new Storage Process, for our example we created the “ZIPQ”:

    Then, assign the external steps to the storage process as follows: the first step handles the quality inspection at the work center, and the second step manages the warehouse task for the final putaway.

    The storage process (POSC) should be assigned to the WPT used for the goods receipt process, following the customizing below

  • SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Warehouse Process Type



MASTER DATA:
After completing the customizing steps some master data must be maintained so the process works. The first step is to maintain and activate the inspection type at the material master (MM02).

  • T-Code: MM02 - Maintain material master data

    Another master data that should be in place is the work center attributes. Here you must maintain the storage bin as follows:

  • T-Code: /SCWM/TWORKST
    Menu: Extended Warehouse Management -> Master Data -> Work Center -> Define Master Data Attributes
    Make sure the storage bin for the Quality Work Center is filled. If it's not, you won't be able to do any follow-up actions after making a usage decision. If you don't, you'll see the following error:

    Message no. /SCWM/QUI_WRKST099 HU 112345678000003606 has no external step for the storage process control assigned

    The last master data step will be creating an inspection rule on the EWM side to trigger the inspection lot creation.

  • T-Code: /SCWM/QRSETUP
    Menu: Extended Warehouse Management -> Master Data -> Quality Management -> Maintain Inspection Rule


TESTING

  • Step 1 – Receiving HU with RFUI
    T-Code: /SCWM/RFUI

    The inbound delivery was created automatically after the HU creation using RFUI:

  • Step 2 – Create WT to QM Work Center
    T-Code: /SCWM/PRDI

    This step can be automated using a PPF action to create and confirm the WT, but in this case, we will perform it manually:

    During the HU-WT creation, we can see that the storage process “ZIPQ” was set according to our customizing:
    NOTE: Confirm the WT using RFUI or manually using foreground confirmation in /SCWM/MON for test purposes.

    After creating the WT, the inspection lot will be automatically created as we can see in the EWM monitor.

  • Step 3 – Step 3: Process Usage Decision
    T-Code: /SCWM/QINSP_S4

    Open the quality inspection work center, select the inspection lot created, and make a usage decision.

    After UD, goods receipt is performed as we can see in the production order:

    Also, the WT to the final putaway is created based on the follow-up actions:
    NOTE: Confirm the WT using RFUI or manually using foreground confirmation in /SCWM/MON for test purposes.

CONCLUSION
Using the Quality Inspection Engine (QIE) within EWM is highly recommended because it is integrated with warehouse processes, providing real-time data and automation that streamlines inspections directly within the warehouse environment. This integration reduces manual effort, minimizes errors, and ensures immediate updates to stock status and quality results

I try to make it simple, hope this guide helps you guys.

Best Regards,

Yuri Nogueira
SAP EWM Consultant
